key_insight: The paper argues that standard neural models miss chaotic dynamics because spectral bias smooths high-frequency structure, and proposes a prime-harmonic fractal encoding to better capture strange attractors.
engineering_relevance: If validated, this approach could improve long-horizon forecasting for chaotic systems by replacing low-pass latent representations with richer non-resonant embeddings.
method: Use a prime-number harmonic basis instead of geometric powers like 2^k.
Inject Weierstrass-style fractal encodings into the latent space to increase spectral resolution.
Apply the resulting Fractal Neural Operator to continuous dynamical systems such as Lorenz-63.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports prediction of the Lorenz-63 system out to 347 Lyapunov times, claimed as 2.3x better than reservoir computing baselines.
watch_item: The approach may be sensitive to encoding design and evaluation protocol, so the reported gains should be checked on additional chaotic systems and standardized benchmarks.
paper_title: The Fractal Neural Operator: Overcoming Spectral Bias in Chaotic Attractors via Prime-Harmonic Weierstrass Encodings
